mission: Protect every OpenClaw instance in the world. Quality bar: staff security engineer with 20 years experience.

tasks.title: --url flag: audit any OpenClaw instance (local or remote)
tasks.why: Without this, Docker/VPS installs get wrong results. The most exposed instances are VPS. We can't claim to protect them if we can't actually probe them.
tasks.acceptance: clawarmor audit --url 10.0.0.5:18789 probes remote, notes local config, shows auth warning for non-localhost

tasks.title: Config path discovery: find the RUNNING instance, not a guessed path
tasks.why: Multi-instance machines audit the wrong config. '100/100 then got hacked' is worse than not shipping.
tasks.acceptance: ps-based discovery finds actual config path and port from running process args. Warns if multiple instances found.

tasks.title: Sandbox fix safety: check mainKey before applying
tasks.why: Custom mainKey + sandbox mode=non-main can break the main session.
tasks.acceptance: fix --apply warns if config has non-default mainKey. Does not block, just warns clearly.

tasks.title: Scan calibration: zero false positives against full ClawHub catalog
tasks.why: One viral tweet showing ClawArmor flagging a legitimate popular skill kills the tool's reputation permanently.
tasks.acceptance: Run scanner against every public ClawHub skill. Manual review of every finding. Zero FP rate confirmed before ClawHub submission.

tasks.title: AST-based JS analysis: catch obfuscated threats
tasks.why: Current grep-based scanner is bypass-able by string concat, encoding, dynamic property access.
tasks.acceptance: Detect: 'ev'+'al', Buffer.from('...','base64'), obj['ex'+'ec']. Using Node.js --input-type parsing, no external deps.

tasks.title: Config integrity hashing: detect tampering between runs
tasks.why: A compromised skill can weaken security by editing openclaw.json. We should catch config drift.
tasks.acceptance: On first clean audit, hash the config. On subsequent runs, detect changes and highlight what changed.

tasks.title: ClawHub submission
tasks.blockedBy: P2-1 (scan calibration must be zero FP first)
tasks.acceptance: Listed on ClawHub. Agents can discover and install via skill system.

tasks.title: HackerNews launch post
tasks.note: Draft at ~/clawarmor/HN_LAUNCH.md. Post after npm is live.
tasks.acceptance: Posted. Links to GitHub. Shows live probe differentiator clearly.

tasks.title: OpenClaw team outreach
tasks.why: If openclaw security audit output references ClawArmor, every new install sees it.
tasks.acceptance: GitHub issue opened on openclaw repo. PR if appropriate.
